paragraphs.body: In my family the way we communicate shows a blend of love and affection. It shapes our identities and values in ways I cannot example. Our conversations are often shown with playful teasing and sharp come backs. My family and friends create an environment where humor becomes a form of connection, and I love it. I would not ask for anything else. It has taught me the importance of toughness and versatility. We can express how we feel while also challenging each other, revealing both our flaws and strengths. It is common for a lighthearted jab to come right after a heartfelt compliment, it is just how we love each other. This language of tension really shows a deep understanding of one another, reinforcing our bonds and teaching us that love is not always soft or sentimental, it can be complex and funny. Through this lens, I have come to appreciate how our interactions reflect our cultural background, where familial love often dances with intensity. This way of affection and tension has informed my own beliefs about relationships, emphasizing that vulnerability and strength can coexist, and that love can be found even in moments of conflict.

annotations.text: My family and friends create an environment where humor becomes a form of connection, and I love it. I would not ask for anything else. It has taught me the importance of toughness and versatility.
annotations.comment: I wonder if an actual story about one of these conversations/moments of linguistic "tension" might more effectively engage your reader and illustrate your point?

paragraphs.body: To live in my house and be a friend in my house means knowing a jab is right around the corner.

annotations.comment: Nice job explaining some of your essay's important ideas. But how can you _show_ your ideas using only concrete and specific language?

paragraphs.body: To speak in my house and be my friend it means knowing that is All love. To live in my house and be my friend it means you Must have tough skin To speak in my house and speak with my friends it means to always add A little Comedic blend to it. Growing Up in this environment has profoundly shaped how I view relationships with people. Like the laughter we share with each other and even the playful jabs my family and my friends take. It has taught me that love is not just all about soft words and gentle gestures, it is about being real with one another or keeping it 100 as me and my friends say. We learned to embrace that we are not perfect and use humor to navigate life's challenges. For instance, I am really really home sick so when I can talk to my mom I talk to her about it and she makes me laugh by saying something like “hey as least you not home with your little brother he is a little shit sometimes” and that laugher clams me down and makes me realize that this distance is only temporary and not forever. But anyways having this approach reminds me that even in challenging times, we can find moments of joy together even when we are across the county. Another example of this is when I'm lonely on a Sunday night because all my teammates go and I'm stuck in my room all by myself I Facetime one and my friends Freddie and his lit hard jabs makes me not so lonely anymore and makes me realize that me going to collage across the country is god's plan for building my character. His humor not only lifts my spirits but also reinforces the idea that these challenges are opportunities for growth and connection, even from a distance.

annotations.text: use humor to navigate life's challenges.
annotations.comment: This is an interesting idea and a repetition. Let's explore it!

annotations.text: “hey as least you not home with your little brother he is a little shit sometimes” and that laugher clams me down and makes me realize that this distance is only temporary and not forever.
Growing Up in this environment has profoundly shaped how I view relationships with people.

nice voice, but can you use more concrete and specific language about what he actually says?

paragraphs.body: Moreover, our way of communicating has instilled in me a deep sense of resilience. I’ve learned that criticism doesn't diminish love because even though my mom calls us little shits, she will always be there for me and my family and if I need anything she will always be there with open arms and that why I say in our family every jab is just a reminder that we love each other. This dynamic has influenced how I relate to friends and colleagues, encouraging them to value open communication and humor in all my relationships. When me and my friends spend time together, we always talk shit to each other like my best friend Isaish he goes to Tennessee and plays football we would always say shit like “aye fatty come over and let's get some food” or “Damm bitch how many candy bars is that.” As I reflect on these experiences, I realize that this blend of love that I have, and tension has become a cornerstone of my identity and is the reason I am the person that I am today. It is a reminder that love can be powerful and complex. In a world where being open or vulnerable can feel risky or scary, my family and friends show me that true connections thrive in honesty and authentic. They create a safe space where sharing our fears, dreams, and imperfections is not only accepted but we celebrated it in a way. Their support reassures me that it is acceptable to be flawed, which inspires me to embrace my own faults. I have discovered in this setting that being open can result in significant development and toughness, supporting the notion that love, and acceptance thrive when we have the courage to be who we really are.

annotations.text: Moreover, our way of communicating has instilled in me a deep sense of resilience.
annotations.comment: What is this voice? This language doesn't sound like the voice you're developing throughout the essay. Also, this move seems to be introducing a new and different idea. Be careful that you're not writing a 5-paragraph essay!

annotations.text: It is a reminder that love can be powerful and complex. In a world where being open or vulnerable can feel risky or scary, my family and friends show me that true connections thrive in honesty and authentic.
annotations.comment: Again, this voice doesn't sound like your own. In fact, it sounds a lot like A.I... ?

annotations.text: “aye fatty come over and let's get some food” or “Damm bitch how many candy bars is that.”
annotations.comment: LOL. I like the authentic voice, but be careful with the profanity (unless it's truly necessary).

paragraphs.body: The distinct communication style within my family and my friends has shaped my view on not only my friends and family but has shaped my view on the world. Humor and honesty have equipped me with the tools to go through life challenges with confidence. In addition to growing resilience, this atmosphere makes me feel like I belong, which enables me to see the beauty in our common flaws. As I move forward with life, I am inspired to create relationships that show authenticity and encourage growth. We can recognize that true connection is based on understanding and acceptance, reminding me that it is perfectly okay to embrace my flaws while finding my true strengths. I have lit the path for deeper and more meaningful connections with people. Because all that life is about building connections with people. That is why these connections give us purpose and meaning to our lives because without purpose in life you arere just living to live and not enjoying the gift of what it means to live LIFE.

annotations.comment: Is this A.I.? As you know, no A.I. of any kind is allowed in our classroom--it's simply not as good as your own voice, especially because it's so vague and general. A.I. basically says nothing specific, or meaningful about your own very real experience, life, and ideas, at all...
Also, your conclusion should echo your intro, which will be more effective if you use an intentional intro form.

There is so much of this draft that is engaging and interesting. I'm talking about those places where you are actually doing the writing. Unfortunately, I think that there are several other places in this essay where A.I. is doing the writing. We'll talk about that. Your own writing, voice, and ideas are so much better than a robot's. You have so many ideas to explore and a plethora of potential meaningful ideas to develop, though I definitely see repetitions, images, moments, etc. coalescing around a couple of different ideas. Let's explore them! Let's talk about your essay's "center of gravity" and the "so what" question so that you can more intentionally develop what's really at the heart of your essay (and then "map" a full set of big ideas). Also, let's talk about how using more concrete and specific details in your poem will help you more intentionally develop and support your essay's big ideas! And let's discuss possibilities for your intro/conclusion. I look forward to a revision.
